div1.entryFree:
Better, adv., compar. of well: Tp. IV, 197. Gentl. V, 2, 18. V, 4, 3. Meas I, 3, 7. II, 1, 268. Ado III, 1, 116. Mids. III, 2, 35 etc. etc. he could never come b. (== more welcome) Wint. IV, 4, 187. makes me the b. to confer with you, Gentl. III, 2, 19.
Better worth == more worth: his health was never b. worth than now, H4A IV, 1, 27. the very train of her worst wearing gown was b. worth than all my father's lands, H6B I, 3, 89. -- To dare b. == to dare rather: dares b. be damned than to do it, All's III, 6, 96. Surrey durst b. have burnt that tongue than said so, H8 III, 2, 253. -- It can be no b. == it must, alas! have been so, Meas. V, 189.
